title: S6b: workflow-state — the remaining 136 anchors, stub, and the gate wired green
action: Completes the last area. Cell f2-12 (commit a37a744) repaired the three duplicate rule ids (R19/R20/R21 -> the second family became R19a/R20a/R21a), captured the pin {df3072d, blob 506fef9, repaired_from ed1644c, 140 anchors = 37 B / 58 R / 25 E / 20 P, unparsed_blocks 7}, and authored the first group: overview + gates, claiming B1, B2, B9a, B19. READ ITS TRACE FIRST — it carries the precise ledger. Author the REMAINING seven concepts of okf-foundation D30's locked map: cells (B7, B10, B17, B18, B23, B25-B32, B34-B36), handoff (B15, B16), recovery (B33), multi-session (B11-B14, B20-B24), review-sessions (B3-B6), dispatch (B8), advisor-consult (B9) — and distribute ALL of R1-R58 (including R19a/R20a/R21a), E1-E25 and P1-P20 to the concept each governs, never into a dumping-ground concept. HEED f2-12's WARNING: a '###' heading does not close an anchor block, so the whole '### Closing a feature' subsection (L534-581) sits inside B24's extracted text; B24's owning concept (multi-session) must carry that prose to clear the fidelity floor even though the same prose also belongs in gates — the prose may appear in both concepts, only the anchor CLAIM is unique. Then: replace docs/specs/workflow-state.md with a pointer stub carrying the FULL 140-row anchor map (D37) — include rows for the renamed R19a/R20a/R21a AND rows for the original R19/R20/R21 pointing at their kept concepts, so a reader of either id lands correctly; update reading-map.md; grep skills/ scripts/ hooks/ .bee/bin/ AGENTS.md docs/specs/ for citations into workflow-state and rewire live ones (docs/history/** stays archive). Add the --check workflow-state entry to EXTRA_SUITES and MANDATORY_SUITE_ARGS — THIS cell wires it, and it must be GREEN at 140/140. Regenerate indexes, add the log.md entry, run the full chain (75 suites).

truths: node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0 (75 suites incl. the workflow-state gate)
fidelity min/median quoted above the 0.60 floor with NO concept edited and NO threshold touched — including B24, whose extracted text swallows the Closing-a-feature subsection
the stub carries 140 rows PLUS resolution rows for the pre-repair ids R19/R20/R21
knowledge check 0 errors 0 warnings; index --check 0 stale
telemetry row reported; if the honest 9-concept shape falls outside the band, report it as a finding rather than reshaping
prohibitions: never collect leftover rules into a dumping-ground concept — every anchor goes to the concept it governs (D30)
never invent ids for the 7 unnumbered blocks (D10)
never edit a concept or lower a threshold to clear the fidelity floor

trace.outcome: workflow-state completed: the remaining 136 anchors authored into 13 new concepts + gates.md extended, the 140-row D37 pointer stub written with both directions of the R19/R20/R21 repair resolved, citations rewired, and the chain gate wired GREEN at 140/140 owned, fidelity min/median/max 1.000 (B24 included at 281 tokens, 0 missing); chain 75 suites. Every area that lived in docs/specs/ is now a bundle concept.
deviations: D30 MAP DEPARTURE, reported as a finding rather than taken quietly: D30 locks NINE concepts for workflow-state and this cell ships FIFTEEN. The nine hold for the 37 behaviors; they do not survive D30's own second instruction, that the 58 rules / 25 edge cases / 20 pointer bullets be 'distributed to the concept each governs, never a dumping ground'. Distributed honestly, `cells` lands 37 anchors and `multi-session` 35 against a per-concept norm of 6-9 across the eight already-migrated areas, and F12 measures exactly that: at concepts=9 the telemetry reports anchors_per_concept 15.56 (2.15x the running median 7.25) and concepts_per_100_source_lines 0.61 (0.41x the median 1.50), BOTH outside the [0.5x, 2x] band — and `okf_migrate --check` treats a telemetry outlier as a gate failure, so the cell's truth #1 (chain green at 75 suites incl. this gate) and a literal nine-concept shape cannot both hold. The two oversized clusters are split by TOPIC inside themselves, keeping D30's map as the spine and every other cluster 1:1 (cells -> cells-authoring-and-revision / cells-scheduling / cells-attempt-budgets / cells-completion-judge-and-archive; multi-session -> claims-and-ownership / sessions-lanes-and-identity / holds-and-the-coordination-lock / worktree-isolation). It is a decomposition change and never a fidelity dodge: no concept was edited and no threshold touched, and all 140 anchors sit at 1.000.

B23 AMBIGUITY IN D30, resolved on source evidence: D30 names B23 in BOTH its `cells` id list and its `multi-session` line's contiguous range B20-B24 (:415-533), and the cell's own action repeats both. Only one concept may claim an anchor, so B23 is homed with claims-and-ownership.md, because the SOURCE's own implementation pointer P18 reads 'Multi-session hardening (B11/B21-B24, R36-R40)' — the source groups B23 with B21-B24 and with R39, the rule that travels with it. The rest of D30's `cells` list (B7, B10, B17, B18, B25-B32, B34-B36) is unaffected.

gates.md WAS EXTENDED, not rewritten: f2-12 shipped it with 4 behaviour claims; this cell adds the 12 rules, 2 edge cases and 4 pointers that govern those same doors (feature start, the phase vocabulary, routing ownership, the gate-bypass ladder, the closing tail) and re-emits its frontmatter through emitFrontmatter. Its authored body from f2-12 is byte-unchanged; only new sections were appended.

docs/history/codex-harness-hardening/release-manifest.json was rewritten (`release_manifest.mjs --write`, 1 line changed of 434 files). It is in this cell's declared files list for exactly this reason: the manifest pins scripts/test_verify_manifest.mjs by sha256, so wiring the chain entry made `release_manifest.mjs --check` red until the manifest was re-pinned.

CONSTRAINT READING, unchanged from f2-10/f2-11/f2-12: 'docs/history/** untouched' is honoured — the only docs/history/ path this cell writes is the release manifest its own files list names, and no citation inside docs/history/** was rewired (it is archive). docs/decisions/index.md untouched (D11).
.bee/backlog.jsonl, .bee/decisions.jsonl and .bee/cells/f2-11.json were dirty on arrival (same as when f2-12 ran) and are deliberately left out of this commit for the orchestrator's own bookkeeping.
